title: Implemented metric that gets biggest function's size
author: InakiVA
description: This metric gets the size of the biggest function.

tests.test_body:
define void @foo(i32 %i, i32 %j, i32 %n) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %i, %j
  br i1 %cmp, label %if.then, label %if.end

if.then:                                          ; preds = %entry
  call void @f()
  br label %if.end

if.end:                                           ; preds = %if.then, %entry
  switch i32 %i, label %sw.default [
    i32 1, label %sw.bb
    i32 2, label %sw.bb1
    i32 3, label %sw.bb1
  ]

sw.bb:                                            ; preds = %if.end
  call void @g()
  br label %sw.epilog

sw.bb1:                                           ; preds = %if.end, %if.end
  call void @h()
  br label %sw.epilog

sw.default:                                       ; preds = %if.end
  call void @k()
  br label %sw.epilog

sw.epilog:                                        ; preds = %sw.default, %sw.bb1, %sw.bb
  %cmp2 = icmp sgt i32 %i, %n
  br i1 %cmp2, label %if.then3, label %if.else

if.then3:                                         ; preds = %sw.epilog
  call void @l()
  br label %if.end4

if.else:                                          ; preds = %sw.epilog
  call void @m()
  br label %if.end4

if.end4:                                          ; preds = %if.else, %if.then3
  ret void
}

declare void @f()

declare void @g()

declare void @h()

declare void @k()

declare void @l()

declare void @m()

comments.author: github-actions[bot]
comments.body:
<!--LLVM CODE FORMAT COMMENT: {clang-format}-->

:white_check_mark: With the latest revision this PR passed the C/C++ code formatter.

comments.author: boomanaiden154
comments.body:
> what's the plan for actually running this in the optimization pipeline so we actually run this code and collect these stats?

It's already enabled by default in the annotation remarks section (along with InstCount). Would be good to figure out if we want this at the beginning or the end of the pipeline and make sure it is where we expect it.

comments.author: boomanaiden154
comments.body:
> ah I didn't realize we added both instcount and function properties stats to the pipeline

We should probably get rid of instcount and just use function properties for everything, although there might be a reason not to do that that I'm not thinking of currently.

comments.author: InakiVA
comments.body: There are currently some instructions in Instruction.def (for instcount pass) that I can't find their counterpart in func-properties. Guess it's just a matter of finding the differences and implementing them on func-properties which is much more robust if they can be
